LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF KERLEY SCHAFFER LLP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR ORDER DISCHARGING STAKEHOLDER-PLAINTIFF FROM LIABILITY AND FOR RELATED ORDERS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DEPOSITED FUNDS IN THE AMOUNT OF $67,031.11 WITH THE COURT AND IS NOW DISCHARGED FROM THE CASE. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: ON ITS OWN MOTION, THE COURT SUA SPONTE GRANTS RECONSIDERATION OF ITS ORDER DISCHARGING KERLEY SCHAFFER LLP FROM THE CASE. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-21-2026 Continued To Sep-02-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify/Clarify Discharge Order As Premature; For Order Requiring Verified Accounting; And For Order Preventing Further Fee/Cost Deductions From Registry Funds
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN HOFMEYER's Motion To Modify/Clarify Discharge Order As Premature; For Order Requiring Verified Accounting; And For Order Preventing Further Fee/Cost Deductions From Registry Funds.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: On its own motion, the court sua sponte grants reconsideration of its order discharging Kerley Schaffer LLP from the case. Kerley Schaffer must secure the appearance or default of the defendants before it may be discharged.

The court denies moving party John Hofmeyer's motion as moot. Moreover, there is no basis to order the accounting and other remedies Hofmeyer seeks.

The court will prepare a written order and serve it on all parties.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

The unopposed motion for discharge of plaintiff Kerley Schaffer LLP is granted. Plaintiff deposited funds in the amount of $67,031.11 with the court and is now discharged from the case.

Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN HOFMEYER MOTION TO TRANSFER

Defendant John Hofmeyer moves to transfer this interpleader action to family court to join In re Marriage of Hofmeyer, San Francisco Superior Court No. FDI-180790640, and seeks other relief. For the reasons stated herein the court denies the motion. (see word minutes for complete ruling)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
